publications.title: Molecular basis of a dominant T cell response to an HIV reverse transcriptase 8-mer epitope presented by the protective allele HLA-B*51:01.
publications.authors: Motozono C, Kuse N, Sun X, Rizkallah PJ, Fuller A, Oka S, Cole DK, Sewell AK, Takiguchi M.
publications.executive_summary: This study determined the crystal structure of a public TRAV17/TRBV7-3 TCR in complex with HLA-B*51:01 presenting the HIV reverse transcriptase 8-mer epitope TAFTIPSI (TI8), the first human TCR–8-mer pMHC structure reported. The dominant clonotype is enriched in HLA-B*51:01–positive patients who remain nonprogressive for AIDS, and the structure reveals that the unusually short peptide does not protrude from the MHC groove; instead, TCR recognition is driven primarily through MHC-centric engagement via CDR1 and CDR2 loops, with a conserved arginine in CDR3α stabilizing the interaction through a ball-and-socket mechanism. This explains the strong bias toward the TRAV17/TRBV7-3 TCR combination, as the specific residues encoded by these genes are essential for recognizing the relatively featureless pMHC surface. The work demonstrates that weak TCR affinity is a characteristic feature of binding to 8-mer peptides presented by MHC class I, suggesting fundamental differences in TCR recognition mechanisms between standard and unusually short peptide antigens.
